channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: U.S. economy created 178,000 jobs in March, a strong gain for a vulnerable labor market
item.description: The unemployment rate edged down to 4.3 percent.

item.title: Pam Bondi. Pete Hegseth. Trump ballroom. ICE arrests. Artemis II. Triplet cyclones. Octopus sex. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Catastrophic sewage spill in Potomac River followed years of delay on repairs, Post review finds
item.description: An investigation by The Washington Post reveals that a prolonged environmental review pushed back work that was initially proposed in 2018. The pipe failed Jan. 19 and released one of the largest spills of untreated wastewater in U.S. history.

item.title: 7 things to know about the end of life, according to death doulas
item.description: They guide people through their final life transition. Here’s what they want everyone to know about dying.

item.title: Hegseth forces out Army’s top general in latest clash between Pentagon and military leadership
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Gen. Randy George to step down and retire, an extraordinary move amid the war with Iran.

item.title: Trump sets 100% drug tariffs on companies that haven’t lowered prices
item.description: Makers of patented pharmaceuticals can escape the import tax by agreeing to join President Donald Trump's “most favored nation” program to reduce U.S. drug prices or by establishing new factories to serve the American market, according to a senior administration official.

item.title: White House ballroom project approved by federal commission packed with Trump allies
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday ordered a halt to the project. The commission’s chair said the ruling didn’t affect the panel’s review.

item.title: Trump ousts loyalist Pam Bondi as attorney general
item.description: The president praised her tenure and said the Justice Department’s No. 2 official, Todd Blanche, will temporarily step into the role. The move comes weeks after Trump dismissed Kristi L. Noem as homeland security secretary.

item.title: Young men are flocking to Catholic churches — for faith, community and girlfriends
item.description: Catholic Mass has become New York’s new hot spot for men in their 20s and 30s who are driven by Gen Z influencers and the eternal search for companionship.

item.title: Oil prices jump after Trump’s speech urging other nations to ‘take the lead’ on reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The price of crude surged around 8 percent Thursday morning and stock markets fell after President Donald Trump in his address Wednesday evening presented no plan to open the critical waterway.

item.title: Trump’s hall-of-mirrors speech on Iran
item.description: The president perpetuated the dissonance that has characterized the Iran war from the start.

item.title: Looping the moon in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Thursday points.

item.title: Ballroom commission changed documents at White House’s request, softening language about its authority over project
item.description: The commission is scheduled to vote today, the final procedural obstacle to building a 90,000-square-foot structure that would dramatically remake one of the most recognized symbols of American power and democracy. A judge earlier this week ordered a halt to construction, a ruling the White House said it will appeal.

item.title: Trump speech. Supreme Court. White House ballroom. DHS shutdown. Social media poll. Spring leaves. Moon mission. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: In address, Trump defends his decision to bomb Iran and says military activity in the Middle East is ‘nearing completion’
item.description: In remarks from the White House, the president said the U.S. was on track to complete all of its objectives in Iran, and that there would be a period of military buildup, though he offered few concrete details. “We're going to hit them extremely hard,” Trump said. “Over the next two to three weeks, we're going to bring them back to the Stone Age.”

item.title: Trump is expected to give an update on the war in Iran in his address to the nation. Follow our coverage.
item.description: President Donald Trump will give his first prime-time speech since the U.S. and Israeli assault on Iran began more than a month ago. Tune in to read insights and analysis from The Washington Post.

item.title: NASA launches Artemis II, its first crewed mission to the moon in more than 50 years
item.description: Four astronauts lifted off just before sunset Wednesday from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The 10-day Artemis II mission is scheduled to loop around the moon for the first time since the Apollo era, a crucial first chapter for an ambitious and risky program to eventually return people to the lunar surface, build a base there and push deeper into the solar system.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis readies launch for historic moon flyby
item.description: A two-hour launch window opens Wednesday evening at 6:24 p.m. at Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral, Florida. If all goes as planned, the 10-day journey will mark the first time humans have left low Earth orbit in more than half a century.

item.title: Risky plan for commandos to seize Iran’s uranium came at Trump’s request
item.description: The mission would require the airlift of potentially hundreds or thousands of troops and heavy equipment to support the excavation and recovery of radioactive material. That could take weeks, former defense officials have estimated, and take place under fire deep inside Iran.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX moves to go public, seeking massive market debut
item.description: SpaceX’s debut marks the most anticipated IPO in years, one that could set a record by making shares of the trillion-dollar firm publicly available. Since its founding in 2002, SpaceX has established a foothold in satellite delivery, taken on numerous missions for NASA, put thousands of satellites into orbit and pioneered reusable rockets.

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship
item.description: The president has argued the 14th Amendment’s guarantee of citizenship does not apply to children born to parents in the country illegally or those on temporary visas for work, travel, school or humanitarian reasons. Most of the justices appeared dubious about that position during more than two hours of arguments, but several also asked sharp questions of an attorney arguing to uphold birthright citizenship.

item.title: Six things to do now to control stuffy noses, itchy eyes and scratchy throats all season long
item.description: Taking action early — even before you notice symptoms — can make it easier to manage your spring allergies. Allergists share their strategies to make the season less debilitating.

item.title: This invention to help older adults is so right, it seems obvious
item.description: A transformative tool is allowing family members to live better lives as they age, Leana S. Wen writes.

item.title: Trump is at the Supreme Court for arguments over birthright citizenship ban, a first for a sitting president. Follow our coverage.
item.description: The Trump administration is arguing that children born to parents without permanent immigration status should not be granted citizenship, upending the long-settled principle that nearly everyone born on U.S. soil is automatically a citizen.

item.title: Markets rally, oil prices fall as Trump prepares to speak to nation on Iran war
item.description: The White House promised an “important update” on Iran, in what is set to be the president’s first prime-time address since the U.S. and Israeli assault began.

item.title: Iran war. Trump’s ballroom. Birthright citizenship. Kid Rock. Federal spending. Allergy tips. Moon mission. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Hegseth exonerates helicopter crews involved in flybys of protesters and Kid Rock, undercutting Army leadership
item.description: The defense secretary nullified the Army’s investigation into unauthorized flybys of the musician’s Nashville home and anti-Trump demonstrations in the state. The highly partisan move was announced hours after military officials opened their review of the soldiers involved. “No punishment. No investigation. Carry on, Patriots,” Hegseth wrote on social media.

item.title: These tiny changes might lengthen your life
item.description: Three manageable tweaks to behavior could have an outsize influence on how long and well we live, a study involving tens of thousands of people shows.

item.title: U.S. Forest Service will move headquarters from D.C. to Utah
item.description: The decision to move to Salt Lake City is part of a broader reorganization that the Trump administration says would move officials closer to forest communities. President Donald Trump had similarly moved the Bureau of Land Management headquarters to Colorado in his first term, leading 90 percent of the staff to leave the agency — only for it to return to Washington.

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s record donation to the National Zoo buys more than an exhibit
item.description: The $51.6 million gift will fund a new exhibit featuring a breeding pair of critically endangered Arabian leopards. The donation also gives the Saudis a soft-power foothold in Washington.

item.title: Federal judge orders halt to construction of Trump’s White House ballroom
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Richard Leon ruled that President Donald Trump lacks the authority to fund the estimated $400 million project through private donations.

item.title: Trump's order eliminating NPR and PBS funding is unconstitutional, judge rules
item.description: The executive order violated the First Amendment rights of the public media giants, a federal judge in Washington found.

item.title: Some of the most popular graduate degrees don’t pay off financially, study finds
item.description: The report found that advanced degrees in medicine, law and pharmacy show the highest return on investment. The study comes at a time when the Trump administration and many education advocates have urged people to carefully consider the cost of graduate programs before enrolling, particularly if they need to take on student loans.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Grandma intent on working family back channels to promote grandpa
item.description: Her son and his family contact the letter writer often, but not his dad and stepmom – so she has someone in mind to fix that.

item.title: Federal panel votes to exempt Gulf of Mexico oil drilling from Endangered Species Act
item.description: The committee — nicknamed the "God Squad" for its ability to decide the fate of species — voted to exempt oil and gas firms from federal wildlife protections, citing the Iran war.

item.title: How risky can the weight-loss drug boom be? I learned the hard way.
item.description: Unregulated copycat versions of GLP-1s are a dangerous blind spot.

item.title: Supreme Court finds ban on gay conversion therapy probably violates free speech
item.description: The major ruling on conversion therapy for minors casts doubt on similar prohibitions in 30 states. It is the latest in a string of decisions by the high court rolling back protections for LGBTQ+ and transgender people.

item.title: A singer’s announcement in today’s On the Record
item.description: Answer now to earn your Tuesday points.

item.title: Our columnist ditched Amazon’s Kindle ecosystem and found an e-reader that would treat him better
item.description: After Kindle removed the ability to download and back up e-books, columnist Michael Coren went in search of an alternative.

item.title: Gas hits $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly four years as Iran continues attacks on energy assets
item.description: A Kuwaiti tanker was struck Tuesday in Dubai’s waters, the latest in a number of attacks that have spiked oil prices and roiled markets. The national average for a gallon of gas was just over $4.01 on Tuesday, according to AAA. The last time that happened was during the inflationary surge of 2022, fueled in part by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: Iran talks. Trump’s ballroom. Kid Rock. Discrimination lawsuit. Graduate degrees. Lifestyle changes. Celine Dion. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: This simple trick can cut your risk of traveler’s diarrhea by 65 percent
item.description: Hint: It’s inexpensive and probably sitting in your medicine cabinet right now.

item.title: Rep. Eric Swalwell sends cease-and-desist letter to FBI to avoid release of files about purported ties to suspected Chinese spy
item.description: The move comes after The Washington Post reported that FBI Director Kash Patel was preparing to release investigative files related to the California Democrat. The Republican-led House Ethics Committee closed a two-year investigation into Swalwell in 2023 without taking any action.

item.title: Celine Dion announces comeback shows four years after diagnosis of rare condition
item.description: The singer, who turned 58 on Monday, plans to perform a set of 10 concerts this fall in France. She had been sidelined from the stage after being diagnosed with an incurable neurological condition known as stiff-person syndrome.

item.title: An insurer canceled a woman’s health coverage over a nickel
item.description: When medical bills started rolling in, a teacher’s aide wondered why her insurance suddenly wasn’t covering them. She discovered, to her surprise, that it had been canceled for “non-payment of premiums.”

item.title: Crypto and private equity would be options for 401(k)s under a proposed Trump administration rule
item.description: The plan is a victory for Wall Street, which has lobbied for wider access to these products, many of which have long been the domain of the wealthy and large institutional investors such as pension funds and insurance companies. Opponents say the products can carry greater risks and higher fees and be harder to buy and sell quickly than typical retirement investments such as stocks and bonds.

item.title: Airport wait times improve as TSA agents begin to get paychecks
item.description: The development is delivering a reprieve to travelers who had contended with hours-long lines and missed flights during a partial government shutdown that has stretched for weeks.

item.title: Protests and cancellations mar Trump administration’s America 250 tour
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon bills her “History Rocks!” events as a nonpartisan celebration of the nation’s 250th birthday, but the program’s ties to a bevy of conservative organizations have fostered an unusual level of opposition.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Stepson’s flirty mom gives their blended family an unwelcome spice
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Stepson’s flirty mom gives their blended family an unwelcome spice

item.title: With a jumper for the ages, U-Conn. freshman Braylon Mullins earned a place in the March Madness pantheon
item.description: With 0.4 seconds left and Connecticut’s season hanging by a thread, the freshman launched a 35-foot prayer that sent top-seeded Duke packing and the Huskies storming back to the Final Four.
